event: escalation of therapy from aminosalicylates to immunomodulators
completed a three-dose induction course of infliximab
latest UC flare was approximately 3 weeks before presentation
treated with IV corticosteroids and another dose of infliximab
ALP at that time was 88 U/L
computed tomography showed multiple multi-loculated abscesses in the right hepatic lobe
extensive intrahepatic thrombosis of the right portal vein
appendix was commented on as being normal
abscess was initially drained by interventional radiology
cultures were positive for skin flora
cultures were positive for Escherichia coli
acid-fast bacilli stain was done on this aspirate and gave a negative result
patient was also bacteremic initially with Streptococcus viridans
patient was bacteremic initially with E. coli
repeat wound cultures showed the same organisms
antimicrobial therapy was changed from vancomycin and meropenem to ceftriaxone and metronidazole
repeat CT (10 and then 20 days after the initial study) showed continued improvement in size of the abscesses
therapy was again de-escalated to oral amoxicillin-clavulanate
discharged on an amino acid-based formula via nasogastric tube and advancing oral diet
liver loculations improved and resolved on ultrasonography by 6 months from discharge
normalization of liver enzymes and inflammatory markers at that time as well
remained without signs or symptoms of UC until approximately 11 months from admission
developed severe pancolitis for which he underwent a total colectomy
surgical gross pathology showed moderately to severely active chronic pancolitis with mural abscesses
mucosa was diffusely tan-red and hyperemic
with scattered diffuse areas of ulceration, nodularity, and pseudopolyps
both the ileum and appendix were without significant abnormalities
no evidence of ischemia, dysplasia or granulomas in any section
